title: Section 8 Company to Private Limited Company Conversion Online
metaDescription: Convert Section 8 company to private limited online. Get expert consultant support, fees, cost in India, documents, compliance & smooth filing service.

hero.headline: Section 8 Company to Private Limited Company Conversion in India (Online)

breadcrumbs.label: Section 8 to Private Limited Company Conversion

hero.subtext: From charity to commerce. Expert-guided Section 8 conversion with full legal compliance.

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: A Section 8 Company (a company formed for promoting commerce, art, science, sports, education, research, social welfare, religion, charity, protection of environment or any such other object), can be converted into a Public Company or a Private Company, in accordance with the Companies Act, 2013. The procedure is prescribed under Rule 21 and 22 of the Companies (Incorporation) Rules, 2014 and is subject to the approval of the Regional Director (RD). Conversion will enable the company to go from a non-profit to a commercial profit-making one. Professionals Law helps you with all your legal, financial & procedural requirements for your conversion like Drafting of Documents, NOCs, RD Approval & Post Conversion Compliances. With Professionals Law, you know you will receive streamlined, certified transition from Section 8 to business-logic legal system.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: Organizations may seek conversion from Section 8 to a private or public limited company when they wish to expand operations into commercial activities, raise equity capital or change their object from charitable to for-profit. Since Section 8 companies are not permitted to distribute profits or dividends, this structure may restrict growth. Professionals Law Private Professionals Law assists companies in the transition, while also managing to adhere to legal approvals, public notices and Regional Director orders through all-in-one compliance, leaving behind the narrow wedge of doing businesses with little and less fear.

body.title: Freedom to Earn and Distribute Profits
body.description: Once converted, the company can legally generate and distribute profits among shareholders, unlike a Section 8 entity.

body.title: Access to Equity Capital
body.description: Conversion allows the company to raise capital through equity shares and attract private investors or venture capital.

body.title: Expanded Business Scope
body.description: The company can now undertake commercial, revenue-generating activities without restrictions imposed by charitable object clauses.

body.title: No Restrictions on Dividend Declaration
body.description: Post-conversion, the company can declare and distribute dividends to shareholders from its profits.

body.title: Better Market Credibility
body.description: Private/Public Companies enjoy higher investor confidence and regulatory recognition in the commercial marketplace.

criteria: The company must be registered as a Section 8 Company under the Companies Act.
It must have a valid reason for conversion and must amend its objectives accordingly.
No outstanding dues should exist with any government body, including Income Tax and GST departments.
It must have filed all annual returns and financial statements with the Registrar of Companies.
A Special Resolution must be passed in a general meeting with member approval.
Consent/NOC must be obtained from creditors, members and stakeholders.
It must not have violated any provisions of Section 8 during its existence.

documents: MOA & AOA of the existing Section 8 Company
Audited Financial Statements for last 2 years
Affidavit confirming compliance with Section 8 norms
Public notice in newspapers (English and vernacular)

body.description: Pass a board resolution approving conversion and fixing an EGM date.
Pass a special resolution with at least 3/4th majority for the conversion.
Publish a public notice of proposed conversion in both English and vernacular newspapers.
Submit Form INC-18 along with attachments to the Regional Director.
Upon review, RD may call for clarifications, then issue conversion approval.
File INC-20 with the ROC along with RD order and altered MOA/AOA.
ROC issues a new Certificate of Incorporation reflecting the new status.
Misrepresentation or concealment of facts during application can attract penalties under Section 447 (Fraud).
Non-compliance with Regional Director’s conditions may lead to rejection or revocation of approval.
Failure to publish the public notice may result in rejection of application.
Operating commercially before approval of conversion may attract fines up to ₹25 lakh and disqualification of directors.

body.title: Document Preparation & Resolutions
body.description: 5 to 7 Working Days

body.title: Newspaper Publication
body.description: 2 to 3 Days

body.title: Filing Application with RD
body.description: 1 to 2 Days

body.title: RD Review & Approval
body.description: 30 to 45 Days

body.title: ROC Filing & COI Issuance
body.description: 5 to 7 Working Days

body.title: Total Time Required
body.description: 45 to 60 Working Days

body.question: Can any Section 8 Company convert into a private or public company?
body.answer: Yes, subject to compliance with Section 8 norms and approval from the Regional Director.

body.question: Is the approval of shareholders required for conversion?
body.answer: Yes, a special resolution passed by shareholders is mandatory.

body.question: Can profits be distributed post conversion?
body.answer: Yes, the company can distribute profits to its shareholders after conversion.

body.question: Is tax applicable during conversion?
body.answer: Conversion is generally tax neutral, but changes in object may require GST/income tax updates.

body.question: Do we need to publish a public notice?
body.answer: Yes, a notice in two newspapers (English and vernacular) is mandatory as per the rules.

body.question: What happens to existing licenses?
body.answer: Licenses must be updated to reflect the new company status post-conversion.

body.question: Can Professionals Law assist with RD approval?
body.answer: Yes, Professionals Law handles end-to-end liaison with the Regional Director.

body.question: Do we need to draft a new MOA and AOA?
body.answer: Yes, a new set of Memorandum and Articles must be drafted to reflect the company’s commercial objectives.

body.question: How long does the process take?
body.answer: Typically, 45 to 60 working days with complete and correct documentation.
